title: Sample
description: One unit of input to an Eval. A Sample carries opaque `input` JSON (whatever the Solver consumes), an optional `target` (the expected output for deterministic scoring), free-form metadata, and an array of attachments referenced by content-hash.

id.description: Stable ULID for this Sample. Sample IDs MUST be unique within a Dataset.

input.description: Opaque input payload. Shape is Solver-defined — could be a string, a chat-message array, a JSON object, etc. Codegen treats this as `unknown`/`Value`/`Any`.

target.description: Optional expected output. Same opaque shape as `input`. Deterministic Scorers compare Solver output against this; if absent, only judge-graded or property-based scorers can run.

metadata.description: Free-form, sample-scoped metadata: source URL, tags, splits, difficulty labels, etc. Open object — `additionalProperties: true` is intentional.

attachments.description: Content-hash references to files associated with this Sample (images, PDFs, code archives). The hash is the canonical identifier; resolution to bytes is the Sandbox/Module layer's job.
